composite.ai_opinion: I agree with the composite SELL direction, as five of eight indicators (62.5%) signal sell pressure, yet the low 40% confidence stems from three neutral signals (Bollinger Bands, RSI, Stochastic) and conflicting MACD divergence strength (0.70) that weakens the overall conviction.

I disagree with the 40% confidence because the composite direction is misleading; while 6 out of 12 indicators signal SELL, the three NEUTRAL indicators (Bollinger Bands, RSI, Stochastic) indicate a lack of momentum confirmation, and the low strength values (0.30–0.31) on the trend indicators suggest the market is in a consolidation phase rather than a definitive downtrend.

I disagree with the 40% confidence level because the composite signal relies on five indicators showing SELL strength (averaging 0.515) while three major trend-following tools (Ichimoku, ADX, EMA 50/200) are either strongly or moderately bearish, yet the overall confidence is artificially low despite the clear majority of technical evidence pointing to a sell.

I disagree with the 40% confidence level because the composite signal is driven by five indicators (65%, 31%, 72%, 80%, 30%) while four others remain neutral, creating a fragmented consensus that suggests market indecision rather than a strong trend.
